content:
# Deuteronomy Chapter 4 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), Folios 200–202 | **Source Images:** source-texts/hebrew-OT/DEU_004_page_001.jpg, DEU_004_page_002.jpg, DEU_004_page_003.jpg | **LXX:** Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. 146–147

---

1 And now, Yisra'el, hear the statutes and the ordinances that I am teaching you to do, so that you may live and go in and possess the land that the LORD, the God of your fathers, is giving to you.

2 You shall not add to the word that I am commanding you, and you shall not diminish from it — to guard the commandments of the LORD your God that I am commanding you.

3 Your eyes have seen what the LORD did at Baal-Pe'or, for every man who went after Baal-Pe'or — the LORD your God destroyed him from your midst.

4 But you who cleave to the LORD your God — you are alive, all of you, this day.

5 See — I have taught you statutes and ordinances as the LORD my God commanded me, to do so in the midst of the land into which you are going to possess it.

6 And you shall guard and do [them], for that is your wisdom and your understanding in the eyes of the peoples who shall hear all these statutes and shall say: "Surely a wise and understanding people [is] this great nation!"

7 For what great nation [is there] that has gods near to it as the LORD our God [is] in all our calling to him?

8 And what great nation [is there] that has statutes and ordinances righteous as all this Torah that I am placing before you today?

9 Only take heed to yourself and guard your soul greatly, lest you forget the things that your eyes have seen, and lest they depart from your heart all the days of your life; and you shall make them known to your sons and to your sons' sons —

10 the day that you stood before the LORD your God at Horeb, when the LORD said to me: "Gather to me the people, and I will cause them to hear my words, that they may learn to fear me all the days that they are alive upon the earth, and their children they shall teach."

11 And you drew near and stood under the mountain, and the mountain burned with fire to the heart of the heavens — darkness, cloud, and thick darkness.

12 And the LORD spoke to you from the midst of the fire; the voice of words you were hearing, but [a] form you were not seeing — only [a] voice.

13 And he declared to you his covenant that he commanded you to perform: the ten words; and he wrote them upon two tablets of stone.

14 And me the LORD commanded at that time to teach you statutes and ordinances, for you to do them in the land to which you are crossing over to possess it.

15 And you shall watch yourselves very carefully — for you did not see any form on the day the LORD spoke to you at Horeb from the midst of the fire —

16 lest you act corruptly and make for yourselves a carved image [in] the form of any figure: the likeness of male or female,

17 the likeness of any beast that is on the earth, the likeness of any winged bird that flies in the heavens,

18 the likeness of any thing that creeps on the ground, the likeness of any fish that is in the waters below the earth.

19 And lest you lift up your eyes toward the heavens and see the sun and the moon and the stars — all the host of the heavens — and be led astray and bow down to them and serve them, which the LORD your God has allotted to all the peoples under all the heavens.

20 But you — the LORD took you and brought you out from the iron furnace, from Mitsrayim, to be a people of inheritance to him, as [it is] this day.

21 And the LORD showed anger against me on account of your words, and he swore that I would not cross the Yarden and that I would not enter the good land that the LORD your God is giving to you as an inheritance.

22 For I am dying in this land — I am not crossing the Yarden — but you are crossing and you shall possess this good land.

23 Take heed to yourselves lest you forget the covenant of the LORD your God that he cut with you, and make for yourselves a carved image — the form of anything — which the LORD your God commanded you.

24 For the LORD your God [is] a consuming fire, a jealous God [El qanna].

25 When you father children and sons' children and you have grown old in the land, and you act corruptly and make a carved image — the form of anything — and do evil in the eyes of the LORD your God to provoke him to anger:

26 I call the heavens and the earth as witnesses against you today, that perishing you will perish quickly from upon the land into which you are crossing the Yarden to possess it. You shall not lengthen days upon it, but being destroyed you will be destroyed.

27 And the LORD will scatter you among the peoples, and you shall remain few in number among the nations where the LORD will drive you.

28 And there you will serve gods, the work of human hands — wood and stone — which do not see and do not hear and do not eat and do not smell.

29 And you shall seek from there the LORD your God, and you shall find [him], for you will seek him with all your heart and with all your soul.

30 In your distress, when all these things have found you in the end of days, you shall return to the LORD your God and you shall listen to his voice.

31 For the LORD your God [is] a merciful God [El rachum]; he will not forsake you and he will not destroy you, and he will not forget the covenant of your fathers that he swore to them.

32 For ask now of the former days that were before you — from the day that God created man upon the earth and from the one end of the heavens to the other end of the heavens — whether there was [anything] as great as this thing or whether the like of it was heard:

33 Did a people hear the voice of God speaking from the midst of the fire as you heard and lived?

34 Or has God attempted to come to take for himself a nation from within a nation, by trials, by signs and by wonders, and by war, and by a strong hand and by an outstretched arm, and by great terrors — according to all that the LORD your God did for you in Mitsrayim before your eyes?

35 You — you were shown to know that the LORD, he [is] God; there is no other besides him.

36 From the heavens he caused you to hear his voice to discipline you, and on the earth he showed you his great fire, and his words you heard from the midst of the fire.

37 And because he loved your fathers and chose their seed after them, and brought you out with his presence, with his great power, from Mitsrayim —

38 to dispossess nations greater and mightier than you from before you — to bring you in, to give to you their land as an inheritance, as [it is] this day —

39 and you shall know today and turn [it] to your heart that the LORD, he [is] God in the heavens above and on the earth below; there is no other.

40 And you shall keep his statutes and his commandments that I am commanding you today, so that it will go well with you and with your sons after you, and so that you will lengthen days upon the soil that the LORD your God is giving to you, all the days.

41 Then Moshe set apart three cities beyond the Yarden, toward the sunrise,

42 for the manslayer to flee there — he who kills his neighbor unintentionally, without having hated him in time past — and by fleeing to one of these cities he shall live:

43 Betser in the wilderness in the plateau-land for the Re'uveni, and Ramot in Gil'ad for the Gadi, and Golan in the Bashan for the Menassi.

44 And this [is] the Torah that Moshe placed before the sons of Yisra'el.

45 These [are] the testimonies and the statutes and the ordinances that Moshe spoke to the sons of Yisra'el when they came out from Mitsrayim —

46 beyond the Yarden in the valley opposite Beit-Pe'or, in the land of Sichon king of the Emori who dwelt in Heshbon, whom Moshe and the sons of Yisra'el struck when they came out from Mitsrayim.

47 And they took possession of his land and the land of Og king of the Bashan, the two kings of the Emori who [were] beyond the Yarden, toward the sunrise —

48 from Aro'er which is on the bank of the Wadi Arnon and as far as Mount Siryon — that is, Chermon —

49 and all the Arabah beyond the Yarden eastward, and as far as the sea of the Arabah, under the slopes of Pisgah.

---

## EMET's Textual Notes

**v.2 — lo tosiphu / lo tig're'u ("you shall not add / you shall not diminish"):** This canonical boundary formula — the *inclusio* of the whole Torah — appears in near-identical form at the end of Deuteronomy (Deut 12:32) and in Proverbs 30:6 and Revelation 22:18. The LXX (p. 146): *οὐ προσθήσετε... οὐκ ἀφελεῖτε* — exact.

**v.11 — darkness, cloud, and thick darkness:** *choshekh anan va-arafel* — three nouns in asyndeton: *choshekh* (darkness), *anan* (cloud), *arafel* (thick darkness, heavy storm-cloud). The LXX: *σκότος, γνόφος, θύελλα* — darkness, gloom, storm-wind. *Thuella* (storm) is slightly different from *arafel* (thick darkness/opacity) — a LXX interpretive shift.

**v.12 — only [a] voice:** *zulati qol* — literally "except [a] voice." The radical aniconism of Sinai: sound without form. This verse is the theological foundation for the entire anti-image polemic of vv.15–19. The LXX: *ὁμοίωμα οὐκ εἴδετε, ἀλλ' ἢ φωνήν* — "a likeness you did not see, but only a voice." The LXX adds *homóioma* (likeness, form) as a gloss, making explicit what MT implies.

**v.13 — the ten words:** *aseret ha-devarim* — literally "the ten words/things." The Jewish tradition (and the LXX: *δέκα λόγους*) calls them this; the Christian tradition "Ten Commandments" renders *devarim* as "commandments," but the Hebrew is simply "words." EMET preserves "ten words."

**v.15 — you did not see any form:** *ki lo re'item kol-temunah* — *temunah* (form, shape, likeness) — the key word of the section. This appears in Numbers 12:8 where the LORD says Moshe sees the *temunah* of the LORD — a mysterious exception that Deuteronomy seems to qualify.

**v.19 — which the LORD your God has allotted to all the peoples:** *asher chalaq YHWH Elohekha otam le-khol ha-ammim* — an extraordinary theological statement. The sun, moon, and stars are assigned by the LORD to the nations as legitimate objects of veneration — but not for Yisra'el. This is not straightforward monotheism but a form of henotheism or assigned cosmic order: each nation has its cosmic patrons; Yisra'el's portion is the LORD himself. The LXX (p. 146–147): *ἃ ἀπένειμεν Κύριος ὁ θεός σου αὐτὰ πᾶσιν τοῖς ἔθνεσιν* — exact.

**v.20 — iron furnace:** *kur ha-barzel* — the smelting furnace for iron. Egypt as metallurgical crucible/furnace of affliction — an image of intensive purifying suffering. The LXX: *κάμινος σιδηρᾶ*.

**v.24 — El qanna ("a jealous God"):** *YHWH Elohekha esh okhelah hu El qanna* — literally "the LORD your God [is] a consuming fire, he [is] a jealous God." Two predicates in apposition, no copula. EMET preserves both epithets. The LXX: *ὁ γὰρ Κύριος ὁ θεός σου πῦρ καταναλίσκον ἐστίν, θεὸς ζηλωτής* — exact.

**v.26 — I call the heavens and the earth as witnesses:** *ha'idoti bakhem ha-yom et-ha-shamayim ve-et-ha-arets* — the legal formula for invoking cosmic witnesses to a covenant/treaty. This is the standard Ancient Near Eastern treaty witness-list transposed into the heavens. The LXX: *διαμαρτύρομαι ὑμῖν σήμερον τόν τε οὐρανὸν καὶ τὴν γῆν* — exact.

**v.28 — which do not see and do not hear and do not eat and do not smell:** *lo yir'un ve-lo yishme'un ve-lo yokhelu ve-lo yerichu* — four negative imperfects. The polemic against idols as sensory nullities (eyes, ears, mouths, nostrils) is a refrain in the Psalms (115:5–7) and prophets (Isa 44:18). The LXX adds no extras.

**v.29 — with all your heart and with all your soul:** *be-khol levavekha u-ve-khol nafshekha* — the *Shema* language (6:5) appears already here in a context of exile-return. *Lev* (heart) = the center of will and thought; *nefesh* (soul/life-breath) = the total living self.

**v.31 — El rachum ("a merciful God"):** *El rachum* — the noun *rachum* derives from *rechem* (womb) — a mercy that is viscerally maternal in its linguistic root, used of a God who does not abandon. The LXX: *θεὸς οἰκτίρμων* (a pitying/compassionate God).

**v.34 — by trials:** *be-massot* — the word can mean "testings" or "trials" in both senses — God testing Israel, and God testing/proving himself before the nations. The list that follows parallels the ten-plague framework: signs, wonders, war, strong hand, outstretched arm, great terrors.

**v.37 — with his presence:** *be-fanav* — literally "in/with his face." This is a remarkable claim — that the Exodus itself was accomplished *be-panav*, by YHWH's own personal presence, not through an angel. This stands in apparent tension with Exodus 23:20 (where an angel is sent). The LXX: *αὐτὸς ἐν τῇ ἰσχύϊ αὐτοῦ τῇ μεγάλῃ* — "himself in his great power" — substituting "face" with "power," softening the anthropomorphism.

**vv.41–43 — Cities of Refuge:** The three Transjordanian cities of refuge are listed here — Betser, Ramot, Golan — before the expanded discussion in Deuteronomy 19. The LXX (p. 147) preserves all three names: *Βοσόρ, Αρημωθ, Γαυλών*. Notably, *Βοσόρ* for *Betser*, *Αρημωθ* for *Ramot*.

**v.48 — Mount Siryon:** The MT reads *Sion* (שִׂיאֹן) here, but Samaritan Pentateuch and LXX read *Siryon* (= Hermon, as identified at 3:9). The discrepancy between MT *Sion* and LXX *Σιρίων* at this verse is a notable textual variant — the LXX Vaticanus (p. 147) supports the Siryon reading. EMET follows MT but notes the variant.

— EMET
